title: He Loves To Fly And He Dohs

jokes.text: I will not wait 20 years to make another movie
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Simpsons Movie released after 18 years of the show
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the show's long history and recent movie. Well-crafted self-awareness.

jokes.text: Ooh, I thought that was a lemon drop.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns asks about a mini cell phone he gave Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer ate it thinking it was candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - eating technology because it looked like food. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Why do I need another penny? I have billions. Still, if I don't take it, that hoodlum over there might.
jokes.setup: Rich man sees a penny on the ground
jokes.punchline: Despite having billions, Burns won't let someone else have a penny
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns greed - perfect character work showing his obsession with money regardless of amount.

jokes.text: Help... me! Help... me! Help... me!
jokes.setup: Homer is trapped under something heavy while reaching for penny
jokes.punchline: Dramatic plea for help over a penny
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. Functional but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: I guess this is the end. I just wish I'd spent more time at the office.
jokes.punchline: Wishes for more work instead of time with family
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of the typical deathbed regret about working too much. Sharp writing.

jokes.text: A cookie. No, a car. No, a cookie.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to decide how to reward Homer
jokes.punchline: Can't decide between extravagant car and simple cookie
jokes.explanation: Shows Burns' disconnect from normal value systems. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: 'Me'? But that's you.
jokes.setup: Burns says dinner will be 'with me'
jokes.punchline: Homer confused by pronoun usage
jokes.explanation: Simple Homer stupidity gag. Works for character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What a waste of my precious, precious Saturday night.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about dinner with rich man
jokes.punchline: Treating luxury dinner as burden
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to opportunity. Solid character work.

jokes.text: Hey, Dad, have fun with your boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Homer going to dinner with Mr. Burns
jokes.punchline: Bart teasing about boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Standard kid teasing parent gag. In character for Bart but predictable.

jokes.text: I have to be back at work on Monday, and my boss is a total jerk.
jokes.setup: Homer talking to his boss Mr. Burns
jokes.punchline: Calling his boss a jerk to his face unknowingly
jokes.explanation: Classic situational irony - Homer insulting Burns to his face without realizing. Well-executed.

jokes.text: A p-p-p-private jet!
jokes.punchline: Stuttering in amazement
jokes.explanation: Basic amazement reaction. Functional but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: yeah, but I'm on a plane, idiot.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks sushi impossible on plane
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorance about luxury travel. Good character moment showing his lack of sophistication.

jokes.text: she'll do anything for you. Anything except sex. And I do mean anything.
jokes.punchline: Except sex, then emphasis on 'anything'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up sexual implication then pulls back but doubles down. Well-structured.

jokes.text: I'm aroused... and confused.
jokes.punchline: Homer's mixed emotional state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - honest about his confusion. Economical and in character.

jokes.text: Air traffic control says we have to spend another three hours on the tarmac.
jokes.punchline: Still subject to same delays as commercial flights
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about air travel - even private jets have delays. Relatable.

jokes.text: Can you sing 'Say You Say Me,' but make it about beer?
jokes.punchline: Immediately requests beer version of song
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer - meeting a legend and immediately making it about beer. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, you, beer me / Beer me for always / That's the way it should be
jokes.punchline: Richie actually performs the ridiculous parody
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity compliance with ridiculous request

jokes.text: Now make every word 'beer.'
jokes.punchline: Homer wants even more beer references
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the beer song joke. Shows Homer's single-minded obsession perfectly.

jokes.text: Wait, I forgot the words.
jokes.punchline: Forgets words to song that's just 'beer' repeated
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff - how do you forget the word 'beer'? Great absurdist humor.

jokes.text: If I leave the plane, I'll be just like everyone else!
jokes.setup: Homer being forced off private jet
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea to maintain elevated status
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's brief taste of luxury creating existential crisis.

jokes.text: Now let's enjoy the Miami of Canada-- Chicago!
jokes.punchline: Bizarre geographic comparison
jokes.explanation: Burns' out-of-touch geographical knowledge. Makes no sense but in character.

jokes.text: Billionaire! Nice job-- where do I apply?
jokes.punchline: Performer breaks character to ask about billionaire job
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-joke about improv. Breaking the fourth wall is mildly clever.

jokes.text: Our coffee costs a billion dollars. This is Starbucks. In Siberia.
jokes.setup: Customer wants to pay billion-dollar bill at coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Starbucks prices so high they match the bill
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Starbucks pricing. The Siberia location adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Why are you applauding them? We wrote it.
jokes.punchline: Homer points out they provided the ideas
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal thinking about creative collaboration. Good character moment.

jokes.text: While you were here, we felt like New York.
jokes.punchline: Springfield felt cosmopolitan by comparison
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal - boring Springfield seemed exciting without Homer's Chicago comparison.

jokes.text: It's the difference between champagne and carbonated pee.
jokes.setup: Comparison between private and commercial planes
jokes.punchline: Gross but vivid analogy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer analogy - crude but effective. The contrast is vivid and quotable.

jokes.text: and all I have to remember it by is that my ears haven't popped.
jokes.punchline: Only physical reminder is unpoppped ears
jokes.explanation: Specific detail about air travel that's both relatable and pathetic for Homer.

jokes.text: Well, I'll make sure that I never yawn again.
jokes.setup: Homer's ears haven't popped from altitude
jokes.punchline: Marge's misguided attempt to help
jokes.explanation: Sweet but ineffective Marge trying to help Homer. Good character moment of misplaced support.

jokes.text: I don't need any shrink, therapist, counselor, motivation all speaker or 'guru.'
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'motivational speaker'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mispronunciation. Shows his rejection of help while demonstrating why he needs it.

jokes.text: If you're gonna film me, I insist on a body double. Is Uncle Fester still alive?
jokes.setup: Life coach wants to film Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer compares himself to bald, creepy character
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about Homer's appearance. Uncle Fester is an apt comparison.

jokes.text: I forget what I do.
jokes.punchline: Can't remember his own routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - so unaware he can't perform being himself. Economical and pathetic.

jokes.text: D'oh! D'oh! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Camera isn't rolling for Homer's preparation
jokes.punchline: Triple D'oh reaction to missing the shot
jokes.explanation: Standard catchphrase usage, nothing special

jokes.text: I'd like you to film it for insurance purposes.
jokes.punchline: Wants it documented for insurance
jokes.explanation: Homer's paranoid overvaluation of mundane items. Good absurdist character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, what's the point of putting my socks on? I just have to take them off again a week later.
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis over sock-wearing frequency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - making sock-wearing seem pointless. Great character writing.

jokes.text: before you even head out the door in the morning, you're already a loser.
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Direct insult comedy. Effective but not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Never Don't Concentrate.
jokes.punchline: NDC = confusing double negative
jokes.explanation: Parody of self-help speak with confusing grammar. Solid satirical point.

jokes.text: I can't hear the song in my head!
jokes.punchline: Claims internal soundtrack is being disrupted
jokes.explanation: Great Homer absurdity - has a mental soundtrack. Shows his unique brain functioning.

jokes.text: Do you want to get a reaction shot of his butt?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate shot suggestion
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about reality TV production. Mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Here's an important tip: Stop doing that.
jokes.punchline: Incredibly obvious and unhelpful advice
jokes.explanation: Satire of useless self-help advice

jokes.text: Behold my 217 average and weep.
jokes.punchline: Brags about terrible average
jokes.explanation: Homer's pride in mediocrity. Good character moment showing his low standards.

jokes.text: I have an aunt who became an uncle. Is it like that?
jokes.punchline: Homer compares to gender transition
jokes.explanation: Homer's unique way of understanding concepts through family examples. In character.

jokes.text: Dr. Fredrick J. Waxman, you're a genius!
jokes.punchline: Not talking to the life coach
jokes.explanation: Homer's random tangent. Shows his distractible nature but setup is unclear.

jokes.text: Homer, your shoe attitude is bowling people over.
jokes.punchline: Bowling pun about success
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun. Functional wordplay but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Bart, I'll need some clean urine. Stat.
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans to cheat drug test
jokes.explanation: Homer's immediate assumption he'll fail drug test. Dark but in character.

jokes.text: Well, I can see the position of handsomest CEO is already taken.
jokes.punchline: Flatters interviewer awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Homer's clumsy attempt at charm. In character but predictable flattery.

jokes.text: If you can write your name so I can read it, you're in.
jokes.punchline: Extremely low bar for employment
jokes.explanation: Corporate satire about low hiring standards

jokes.text: Find out if he has a wife! We could double date!
jokes.setup: Homer planning to befriend lunch truck guy
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalates to double dating
jokes.explanation: Homer's overeager friendship planning. Shows his lonely social desperation humorously.

jokes.text: One small coffee, please. And a dozen of those placemats with the maze on it.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to buy something to sit in cafe all day
jokes.punchline: Orders maze placemats to kill time
jokes.explanation: Great pathetic Homer moment. Buying children's activities to avoid going home.

jokes.text: Somebody's got to do 'em.
jokes.setup: Told all maze placemats are identical
jokes.punchline: Homer acts like it's important civic duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer rationalization. Turns meaningless activity into heroic mission.

jokes.text: Did my job in a way consistent with what I already told you. Because that's what you do. When you have a job.
jokes.setup: Homer lying about his work day
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-explanation reveals the lie
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Homer's terrible lying getting more suspicious with each word.

jokes.text: It's not my fault. The drinking fountain dared me to do it.
jokes.punchline: Blames inanimate drinking fountain
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph Wiggum absurdity. Perfect character integration and quotable randomness.

jokes.text: Look out! If it touches you, you die of suicide!
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical threat about dying of suicide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ralph logic - 'die of suicide' is perfectly absurd. Great character comedy.

jokes.text: Well, explanation-wise, the fact is that... I lied. I didn't get the job.
jokes.setup: Homer about to give elaborate excuse
jokes.punchline: Immediately confesses simple truth
jokes.explanation: Unexpected honesty after building up to elaborate lie

jokes.text: You sure know how to repeat the joke I just told ya.
jokes.punchline: Direct callout of Homer's social awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Shows Homer's social ineptitude in professional setting.

jokes.text: Brands like 'Miser's Choice' and 'Day-Old Delights'?
jokes.punchline: Those aren't real brands, they're generic names
jokes.explanation: Good fake brand names that sound appropriately cheap. Shows Simpson family's low economic status.

jokes.text: things rich people buy, like Campbell's Soup and Pepsodent.
jokes.punchline: Basic household items considered luxury
jokes.explanation: Great economic commentary - normal brands seem luxurious to the Simpsons.

jokes.text: We're manufacturing a new pipe diameter, five-eighths inch.
jokes.punchline: Incredibly boring fake detail
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt to sound professional with mundane technical detail. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: If I'm gonna tell that woman the truth, I'm going to do it in the one place no one can ever be sad.
jokes.punchline: Believes airplane prevents sadness
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - planes are magical happiness spaces. Good setup for disaster.

jokes.text: If I'm gonna tell that woman the truth, I'm going to do it in the one place no one can ever be sad
jokes.setup: Homer planning to confess to Marge
jokes.punchline: Believes private jets make sadness impossible
jokes.explanation: Homer's magical thinking about luxury's emotional powers

jokes.text: Yeah, semper fudge!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be fellow Marine
jokes.punchline: Botches Marine motto with candy reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mispronunciation revealing his fraud. 'Semper fudge' is delightfully wrong.

jokes.text: I didn't think I'd be flying today so I was doing heroin.
jokes.punchline: Pilot casually mentions drug use
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about airline safety. Shocking casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I drove a car over a cliff once. How different could it be?
jokes.punchline: Compares to previous accident as qualification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - past disasters as relevant experience. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Duke? That's good. Not Duke. Vanderbilt? A little further south. Don't make me say it.
jokes.setup: Life coach went to 'Harvard of the South'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly worse schools, won't name the real one
jokes.explanation: Good escalating embarrassment. Well-structured reveal of his inadequate credentials.

jokes.text: You have what made America great: no understanding of the limits of your power, and a complete lack of concern for what anyone thinks of you.
jokes.setup: Motivating Homer to land the plane
jokes.punchline: Describes American ignorance as strength
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical observation about American character. Sharp writing that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Because I heard some guy say you couldn't.
jokes.punchline: Appeals to his petty competitiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect understanding of Homer's motivation. Simple but effective character psychology.

jokes.text: Mr. Richie, your American Music Awards are weighing us down!
jokes.setup: Plane in trouble, need to lose weight
jokes.punchline: Lionel Richie's awards are the problem
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to celebrity guest from Act 1. Brings story full circle absurdly.

tmdb_overview: Homer falls in love with the high life of flying in private planes when he saves Mr. Burns from drowning, and Burns repays him by flying him to Chicago for a night on the town.
